notes: Hand-curated question bank by the project author. Plain English. Reference only.

questions.text: What records should I bring (prior labs, imaging, hospital discharge summaries)?
Should I fast or hold any medication before this visit?
Will I need a ride home (sedation, dilating drops)?
What is the working diagnosis or differential?
What is the goal of today’s visit?
What are the warning signs that should prompt me to call or go to the ER?
Are there lifestyle changes you would prioritize first?
What is each medication for and how long should I take it?
What side effects should I watch for and which require a call?
Are any of my current medications duplicated or interacting?
Is there a generic that costs less?
What test is being ordered and what would the result change about my care?
Is this test urgent? When should I expect results and how will I be notified?
Are there safer or less expensive alternatives to this test?
Is this provider in-network for my insurance today?
Can I have a Good Faith Estimate in writing if I am uninsured or self-pay?
Will labs or imaging be billed by a separate facility?
When and how should I follow up with you?
What instructions should I share with caregivers or family?
Where can I get reliable information to read more on this?
What pregnancy milestones or screenings are next?
How is my child’s growth and development tracking against expected milestones?
What coping strategies or therapy options should I consider alongside medication?
What is the recovery timeline and when can I return to work, exercise, driving?
